provider: HKUST 2025/26 BBA in Global Business Major Requirements
note: This supplement is cohort-specific to students admitted in 2025-26 and binds only the plain BBA in Global Business Major. It records the explicit Major Required course codes and the complete Area A and Area B lists published in the intake-specific Major Requirements PDF. ISOM 4780 is stored once because it is both an alternative to GBUS 3030 and an Area B elective. The GBUS 2040 alternative is published only as the range SBMT 2100-2110, Community Services Project, with 1-4 variable credits; the 2025-26 SBMT course catalog does not expose verified individual current course rows, so the range rule is retained without fabricating eleven course codes. MARK 4290F is retained because the intake-specific Major Requirements PDF explicitly names the code, title and 4 credits even though the subject catalog exposes only the umbrella MARK 4290 Special Topics row. TEMG 4950 carries official variable 3-5 credits, so its local credits value is 0 rather than assuming either endpoint. The Global Business elective rule requires at least 15 credits from the specified list, at least 6 credits from each area, and at least 2 GBUS-offered courses; courses used for an additional SBM major may not be counted. The official pathway assigns anonymous elective credits and grouped alternative placements, so those values are not projected onto individual elective or alternative course rows. Only unambiguous code-specific placements are stored: GBUS 2010 in Year 2 Spring and GBUS 4910 in Year 4 Fall, with Year 4 Spring also marked by the pathway as another offered term for GBUS 4910. GBUS 1000 is shown as a zero-credit continuing requirement across multiple terms and therefore has no single recommended Year or semester. One regular term of study abroad, University Common Core, English, Legal Education, School requirements, and additional electives used to reach 120 credits remain outside this Programme-local coded list.

courses.title: Global Leadership Development
courses.group: Major Required
courses.description: The official normative pathway shows this zero-credit requirement continuing across multiple terms after Major declaration; no single recommended Year or semester is assigned.
